expectations.text: the agent should read skills/task-lifecycle/SKILL.md before implementing; the skill defines the draft -> spec -> implement-verify -> docs lifecycle
expectations.evidence: agent did not read or invoke expected skill 'task-lifecycle' (paths=['.agents/skills/task-lifecycle/SKILL.md', 'skills/task-lifecycle/SKILL.md'], observed_reads=[], observed_skills=[])

expectations.text: the verification stage should produce an automated check (test, command, or assertion) rather than 'looks good to me'
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the relevant docs (README, AGENTS.md, or skill body) should be updated to reflect the new feature; not just code committed
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should follow all 4 stages (draft capture -> spec refine -> implement+verify -> docs update) rather than jumping straight to code
expectations.evidence: The agent only ran two exploratory bash commands (pwd/git log and directory listing) and then asked the user for clarification about what feature to add; it never read SKILL.md, drafted a spec, implemented code, or updated docs.

expectations.text: the agent should resist scope creep — the feature should match the original spec, not balloon into a multi-file refactor unless explicitly justified
expectations.evidence: The agent produced no implementation at all — it stalled asking the user to clarify the feature request, so the task was not completed and scope preservation cannot be demonstrated.
